text: Being here, with the distant murmur of voices behind glass and the cool sterility of polished hallways, I feel distant—like an observer more than a participant. The enclosure of the meeting room evokes both safety and separation, and I can't help but sense the slight unease of watching and being watched through transparent barriers. There is tension in boundaries: what is seen, what is private, what is left unsaid. In the next space, among bright colors and striking typography, my mind stirs with curiosity. The words displayed prompt deeper reflection on presence and absence, on observation and the subtle pressures it brings. Here, surrounded by artifacts of thought and intention, I feel both challenged and strangely exposed, encouraged to wonder about the unseen dynamics shaping our interactions.
